revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:ヒシアゲ古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Hishiage Kofun | image = File:Hishiage.JPG | caption = Worship area at Hishiage Kofun | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11383489|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.701314|N|135.802689|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | built = Mid to late {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | burial = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|qid=Q1066593|lt=Princess Iwa|1=Princess Iwa}} | area = Length 219 meters
Height 16.2 meters | burial goods = | burial Management = {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} "Heijō Sakaue burial Site" | notes = 24th-largest kofun in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Hishiage
Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Princess Iwa|qid=Q1066593|1=Princess Iwa}} }} == Overview == The '''Hishiage Kofun''' is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}}. Although the actual individual interred is unknown, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates the mound as the burial site for {{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|qid=Q1066593|lt=Princess Iwa|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, under the name "'''Heijō Sakaue burial Site'''" (平城坂上陵). == Description == Located in Saki-chō, Hishiage, Nara City, the tumulus measures 219 meters in length, with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 124 meters * Rear circular section height: 16.2 meters * Front section width: 145 meters * Front section height: 13.6 meters The tumulus is under the management of the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} and is not open to the public. Its construction is believed to date back to the mid to late 5th century. == burial == [[File:Hishiage-kofun.jpg|200px|left|thumb|Partially reconstructed outer embankment]] The tumulus is notable for its double moat at the front section. In 1993, the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|qid=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} conducted a survey that uncovered a row of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} near the outer edge of the inner embankment on the eastern side of the rear circular section. Restoration efforts have partially reconstructed the site, and replicas of haniwa are now displayed. A bridge-like earthen embankment crossing the outer moat was also identified.Fumiaki Imao, "Yamato Governance and the Saki Kofun Cluster," pp. 74–75. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大仙古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}} — Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as the burial site of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|version=93664964|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:ヒシアゲ古墳]] {{Commons category|Hishiage Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11330004}} [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
